title: 'Midweek Melodies' Free Happy Hour Concert
description: Free live music is back in the park. Illuminate LIVE returns to the Golden Gate Bandshell in Golden Gate Park on Sunday, March 1, 2026, launching its sixth season of free outdoor concerts with more than 125 performances scheduled through mid-November. The long-running series has quietly become one of San Francisco’s biggest free music traditions, drawing more than 250,000 attendees each year. Since 2020, Illuminate LIVE has presented more than 700 free concerts and welcomed nearly 1 million music fans to the historic Bandshell (also known as the Spreckels Temple of Music). The lineup spans R&B, soul, jazz, funk, big band, string ensembles, punk, indie and psychedelic rock, singer-songwriters, reggae, Latin and electronic music, with a strong emphasis on Bay Area talent. Originally launched during Golden Gate Park’s 150th anniversary, Illuminate LIVE has grown into a cornerstone of the city’s free public arts scene, supported by private funding, grants and partnership with the San Francisco Recreation and Park Department.

title: Songs from a Sinking Ship
description: Flamenco Arts International (FAI) presents the World Premiere of Songs from a Sinking Ship — two performances only, May 23, 2026 at 2 p.m. and 7:30 p.m. at the Presidio Theatre. The production follows a ship's crew as a mysterious siren's warning transforms a routine voyage into a supernatural battle between ego and redemption — all told through flamenco. Thunder, sea, and the supernatural come alive through passionate dance, original music, and immersive performance. Songs from a Sinking Ship earned a Special Award from the Isadora Duncan Dance Awards for outstanding achievement in every aspect of performance, along with Izzie nominations for Outstanding Achievement in Direction or Choreography and Outstanding Achievement in Performance – Company. Directed by José Maldonado. Featuring Juan José Amador, Eugenio Iglesias, Carlos Menchaca, David Chupete, Marian Fernández, Reyes Martín, Marina Elana, and living legend El Torombo.

title: Ferry BuildingFarmers Market
description: With over 100+ vendors, the Saturday market is held both in front of the Ferry Building, along the Embarcadero, and on the rear plaza overlooking the Bay. The market offers a wide variety of fresh, local produce, food products, and brunch and lunch options. Stop by the Foodwise Classroom for free cooking demos.

title: Monet and Venice
description: Although Claude Monet visited Venice only once, his paintings of the city are among his most dazzling. This exhibition, co-organized with the Brooklyn Museum, is the first dedicated to Monet’s Venetian cityscapes since their debut over a century ago. Featuring more than 100 artworks, the exhibition places Monet’s Venice paintings alongside select works from across his career, including his Water Lilies, as well as Venetian views by artists such as Renoir, Sargent, and Canaletto. Unlike the bustling scenes painted by other artists, Monet’s Venice is eerily deserted, its architecture, buildings, and canals dissolving in an encompassing, hazy light he described as the enveloppe. This exhibition offers a rare opportunity to experience Monet’s vision of the famed Italian city.
